title: Colour symbolism in Rebuild
wikitext:
[]: Colour symbolism in Rebuild             Quote Post by Combs » Wed Dec 08, 2010 1:11 am I've read and skimmed through discussions about colour and it's symbolism in the original series. But recently I've been wondering about how it's been extended into the movies, and what we can extract from oddities that arise. For many times we've seen the comparison Arise with Rei, Blue, and the Moon. SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Colour symbolism in Rebuild             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Dec 08, 2010 7:46 am Combs wrote: I think it's likely a burst of a seed of life, but the point here is that Rei's symbol has been defiled red, Asuka's red. Is this an explanation for Rei's changed, more open attitude as of 2.0?

[]:             Quote Post by Marduk » Wed Dec 08, 2010 8:10 am And here I thought somebody was bringing up the "Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse" color reference. Carry on then!

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Wed Dec 08, 2010 8:40 am As far as NME is concerned, the Moon is Kaworu's symbol. There is that shot of Rei('s ass) before Operation Yashima, but Kaworu wakes up on the Moon, spent most of his time so far on the Moon, and when he arrives to Earth at the end of 2.22, it's nightfall with the full Moon on the sky. At this point, they could call him "Moon Kid", and it wouldn't feel too silly. What this may signify beyond some neat visuals? I don't know. Probably just a way to give Kaworu some extra points in his "being unsettling and alien" skills.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Dec 08, 2010 3:15 pm Colors, particulary seven of them, make an intresting appearance in form of a rainbow. Since Gendo & co. have a 'convenant' with Lillith (=something like God) the Rainbow could be a reference to the biblical story of Noah where the Rainbow is a sigh that there will be no horrible catastrophy/flood ever again. (The SI as previous 'flood'? -> the red oceans, Tsunamis associated with SI --> Rainbow (=Destruction of an angel) as a sighn that there won't be another calamity to decimate the lillim? Also there's one when Shinji falls over Rei. It was already in the original, and there wasn't much interpreted into it, but old NGE didn't use any rainbow symbolism. Could that Rainbow mark Shinji 'deafeating' the second angel (i.e. Lillith) by beginning his connection to Rei that would eventually stop her from destroying the world? Oh, and Shikinami is seen wearing more Red/pink than Soryu did even in her normal outfits (Rei's uniform is blue/white anyway) that might be to increase the contrast/make up for the sad absence of Blue!Zerogoki. you think not changing it to blue has a symbolism? As for Kaworu and the moon: It always a bone white full moon White moon -> Adam -> Kaworu

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Wed Dec 08, 2010 3:56 pm The only one so far i've see are the gloves used for the dvds, with i think may be representation of rainbow,red and orange, i suspect next would be either yellow or blue.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Dec 08, 2010 4:55 pm Kendrix wrote: you think not changing it to blue has a symbolism?

[]:             Quote Post by 1731298478 » Wed Dec 08, 2010 9:44 pm Kendrix wrote: Colors, particulary seven of them, make an intresting appearance in form of a rainbow. Since Gendo & co. have a 'convenant' with Lillith (=something like God) the Rainbow could be a reference to the biblical story of Noah where the Rainbow is a sigh that there will be no horrible catastrophy/flood ever again. (The SI as previous 'flood'? -> the red oceans, Tsunamis associated with SI --> Rainbow (=Destruction of an angel) as a sighn that there won't be another calamity to decimate the lillim?

[]:             Quote Post by Chrad » Wed Dec 08, 2010 11:22 pm The climax of 2.0 struck me as using the same red/blue coding that NGE and particularly EoE used. There's cutting between red as the outside world (reality) goes to shit and blue as Shinji moves into his comfy womb of isolation.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Dec 09, 2010 3:43 am Chrad wrote: The climax of 2.0 struck me as using the same red/blue coding that NGE and particularly EoE used. There's cutting between red as the outside world (reality) goes to shit and blue as Shinji moves into his comfy womb of isolation.

[]:             Quote Post by Chrad » Thu Dec 09, 2010 7:43 am Xard wrote: expect the whole place was black void and Shinji was going crazy with colours flashing on his face and skin being ripped off painfully etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Thu Dec 09, 2010 8:37 am The climax of 2.0 struck me as using the same red/blue coding that NGE and particularly EoE used.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Thu Dec 09, 2010 10:09 am Jayfive wrote: And its THAT shade of red. The one that is EoE shorthand for 'oh crap, we're in trouble now'.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Dec 09, 2010 11:25 am Kendrix wrote: Also there's one when Shinji falls over Rei. It was already in the original, and there wasn't much interpreted into it, but old NGE didn't use any rainbow symbolism.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Dec 09, 2010 4:50 pm Kendrix wrote: Oh, and Shikinami is seen wearing more Red/pink than Soryu did even in her normal outfits (Rei's uniform is blue/white anyway) that might be to increase the contrast/make up for the sad absence of Blue!Zerogoki. you think not changing it to blue has a symbolism?

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Fri Dec 10, 2010 7:10 am Sachi wrote: I think reserving Unit-00's yellow color has a lot to do with the fact that the writers were planning on having a blue Evangelion eventually (Unit-07, I think?)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Dec 10, 2010 9:37 am SaltyJoe wrote: The main color of the Mark.06 is a particularly dark shade of blue, so i guess that's already accounted for.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 10, 2010 12:05 pm Kendrix wrote: I always thought it was black.... (except for the yellow stripes, of course)

[]:             Quote Post by CORE » Fri Dec 10, 2010 3:15 pm SaltyJoe wrote: As far as NME is concerned, the Moon is Kaworu's symbol. There is that shot of Rei('s ass) before Operation Yashima, but Kaworu wakes up on the Moon, spent most of his time so far on the Moon, and when he arrives to Earth at the end of 2.22, it's nightfall with the full Moon on the sky. At this point, they could call him "Moon Kid", and it wouldn't feel too silly. What this may signify beyond some neat visuals? I don't know. Probably just a way to give Kaworu some extra points in his "being unsettling and alien" skills.

[]:             Quote Post by Combs » Thu Dec 23, 2010 12:23 am I'm quickly coming to believe that Misato represents Venus, and Mari represents Mars. Edit: here, have an Eva solar system. Asuka, Kaworu, Misato, Shinji (Rei), Mari, Gendo, Ritsuko, Naoko, Toji, Mana
